meaning: n.专制主义者,专制政治论者,绝对论者
examples: This absolutist belief is replaced by an appreciation that rules can vary.
One might, for example, be an Absolutist about liberal and a Relativist about vocational education.
We see that, above all, in how the recent debate has been obscured by two opposite and absolutist ends.
In the same period, the reformism of the sixties became, in some quarters, a stern, absolutist enterprise.
The author also grasps how crucial England's political, anti- absolutist traditions were to the development of American democracy.
